Title: Design and implement GenericGatherGoal — arbitrary item gathering including mods
Description:
Replace hardcoded GatherWoodGoal with a generalised gathering system that works for any Minecraft item, including modded items.

The user's intent: 'more agent defined than GatherWood, as it would be nice if it could work with arbitrary items (including mods). I don't know if the llm would have to help architect what that gather task means, but this is something to consider and work towards'

Design questions (run council review before implementing):
1. Item specification schema — what does the agent need to know about an item? { id: 'minecraft:iron_ore', displayName: 'Iron Ore' } minimum. For smelting chains: { id: 'minecraft:iron_ingot', raw: 'minecraft:iron_ore', requiresSmelting: true }
2. GenericGatherGoal(itemSpec, count) vs separate goal classes
3. ItemRegistry — MemorySmith wiki pages at 'item-registry/{itemId}' with block ID, mining tool, crafting chain
4. LLM role — 'gather iron' → LLM resolves to iron_ore, creates GatherItemGoal
5. Mod support — agent cannot know mod item IDs without being told; architecture: MemorySmith pages teach the agent

Suggested implementation path:
1. Rename GatherWoodGoal -> GatherItemGoal(itemId, count) — configurable item ID
2. Keep GoalFactory['GatherWood'] as convenience (creates GatherItemGoal('minecraft:oak_log', 10))
3. Add ItemRegistry backed by MemorySmith SearchMemory('item-registry/{id}')
4. LLM fallback for unknown items (Phase 4+)

See handoff doc for full design analysis: Data/Pages/Tasks/agent-handoff-20260616.md

Comments.Author: AI Agent
Comments.Body: User note: 'I don't know if the llm would have to help architect what that gather task means, but this is something to consider and work towards'. The architecture council should review the ItemRegistry design before implementation. Key risk: mod item IDs are unknown at compile time — the agent must learn them from MemorySmith pages or from the LLM.
